By week 3 more than half the class didn't come to lecture because you gain more by not being there than by listening to his scattered thoughts. He says the same things each lecture and his slides are either just a title or just a block of text.

I wish he taught more philosophy courses. He does go off on tangents but it is not detrimental to the overall understanding of the materials he records his lectures so you're not required to attend them

Student review: Am taking a one unit, SU undergraduate seminar on ISIS and the Islamic State.  Great class.  Gelvin started by giving background, then we read and discuss several articles each week. Gelvin guides discussion lightly and tries to get everyone involved.  Lightens the mood with humor--the subject is stressful.  Take him--you'll learn a lot.

Student review: Funny, smart, approachable.  Teaches ME history from a world history viewpoint ("In the 19th century Ottoman sultans looked at France, looked at China, and decided it would be better to a strong power like France than one picked apart like China").  More interested in the big picture than factoids.  Went to class, took notes, and did fine.

Used to hang out during his office hours, where we talked about course stuff and current events.  He does seem to like undergraduates, but cuts off conversations when you whine about grades.  Overall, I learned alot.  Will take again
